steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that despite Bogotá residents having closer parks, their park usage rate is lower. This contradicts a simple proximity explanation, implying another factor must drive the difference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It was much larger than the researchers conducting the study expected.: Frames the finding as surprising relative to expectations, but the passage is making a logical point about causation, not about what researchers expected. ✗
  • Choice B — It was calculated using sources that predate the survey.: Brings in the origin of the data, which the passage never raises as an issue. ✗
  • Choice C — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents.: Directly captures the passage's logic — proximity alone doesn't explain the gap, so something else does. ✓
  • Choice D — It could be due to inaccuracies in the survey results.: The passage treats the usage data as valid; it draws a conclusion from it rather than questioning it. ✗
That matches Choice C — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents..
